result.summary: Cloudflare exposes a public unauthenticated site API at https://www.cloudflare.com via https://cloudflare.com/openapi.json, an authenticated REST API at https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4 and GraphQL Analytics API at https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/graphql using either `Authorization: Bearer <API token>` or legacy `X-Auth-Key` + `X-Auth-Email`, plus managed MCP servers at `https://mcp.cloudflare.com/mcp` (OAuth or bearer API token) and product-specific MCP endpoints under `*.mcp.cloudflare.com/mcp` with auth not fully documented.

cf_api_token.label: Cloudflare API token
cf_api_token.setup: In the [Cloudflare dashboard](https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens/), go to **My Profile** → **API Tokens** (or **Manage Account** → **API Tokens** for account-owned tokens), click **Create Token**, choose a template or custom token, scope it to the needed account/zone permissions and resources, then copy the token secret shown once. Cloudflare recommends API tokens over Global API keys. Docs: [Create API token](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/get-started/create-token/).

cf_global_api_key.label: Cloudflare Global API key + account email
cf_global_api_key.setup: In the [Cloudflare dashboard](https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens/), go to **User Profile** → **API Tokens** and in **API Keys** click **View** for **Global API Key**. Your account email must be verified first. This is a legacy auth method with full account access; Cloudflare recommends using [API tokens](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/get-started/create-token/) instead. Docs: [Get Global API key (legacy)](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/get-started/keys/).
